user_message: Need help with Wide World Importers -- triage this. This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 12:57 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-27 16:54 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers account health

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-02-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Wide World Importers)

[00:02:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:08:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:10:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:13:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:22:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:26:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:30:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:33:00] Priya (Wide World Importers): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
